reviews.review_title: Best game I play every day
reviews.review_description: I really do like the game. It was really fun when I started playing. I was kind of confused when I started playing, but for all ages I approve. Don’t listen to those one star reviews. They are not true. Only there is two skate park no, there’s not two. There are a bunch of skate parks that you can write on. It is really hard. I really do. It’s not that hard, I really do like the master hall. That’s my favorite well this game was one of my favorites five stars
When I first played it was hard and the controls are confusing but i got used to it so not bad

You can't control your skater to save it's own life in this game the controls are horrible. Half the time I'm skating and then it reverts going the other way. The head tracking for grinds is garbage. No wonder it was on sale for $5 smh but it gave me hopes to one day there will be an official playable vr skate game. Can't wait.

It's a shame this game doesn't get the credit it deserves. I get it's not for everyone, but if it hits for you, it's really amazing. If you're a skateboarder past or present, a fan of games like session and skater xl, then this game is for you. The controls take some time to get, but once you do, the feeling is rewarding, and it's what makes it a fun experience. Also, I have no idea how, but I never get motion sick from this game, and I am prone to it in other games. This is a top 3 vr game for me, and I mostly wanted the devs to know I appreciate what they created.

reviews.review_title: Very fun game but needs outdoors.
reviews.review_description: The overall mechanics of this game are fun. But every single map feels like youre locked in. There needs to be an open map. A variety of open maps really, because while the mechanics are fun the game gets boring without mal variety.

The premise is admirable and innovative, kudos to the developer for the willingness to tackle a difficult experience to emulate, real life street skating. As the game is of this review, it's not worth the price however. The controls are stiff and overly complex and doesn't give the best sensation of the flow of setting up for a line that it could, it's a very awkward and tenuous feeling position to need to constantly keep dialed in.  I do like the use of the grips for turning though, this could be made to work with a different structure of positioning for a much more smooth and simulated flow of maneuvering on your board as in real life in the future for a novel design and would be a great change.  You can't see what your feet are doing and the board past the Ollie is disconnected from the virtual space relevant to the way your upper body reacts during tricks and this is the major issue for me keeping it from being an enjoyable game.  No satisfaction in doing the actual skateboarding in-game due this disconnecting element.

developer_response.body: We're sorry you're struggling with VR skater: SL. It is a game that takes some time to learn, but once the controls click it feels awesome.  We'll look into your feedback as it sounds like a boundary issue with you potentially moving forward when trying to perform tricks? Also you cannot see your feet as the controllers are your feet instead, therefore you just need to know the right actions to perform.

reviews.review_title: Ran Out my Q3 Battery/Ex Again!
reviews.review_description:
Initially I completed the tutorial twice and for some reason couldn't wrap my head around some of the advanced tricks. I really did have to practice to train my mind to grind/slide on rails. Now that the muscle memory is built, I honestly can't get enough of this game!

There were a lot of smart decisions here, from grinds that actually scratch the paint of your board, to having to complete all missions in a run to get the gold. The board customization is fantastic and trophies give a true feeling of accomplishment. There are also Achievements which I absolutely love to hunt. 

The combo tricks bring me back to the original Tony Hawk PS days of aiming for massive scores. There is also a fun bonus level to unlock! 

The ONLY thing I was looking forward to was more variety in the levels. If there was an outdoor beachside level with a day/night cycle, I don't think I would ever leave this game. Even a school yard, mall, or a collab with another VR game like Wallabout, Brink, etc. would make this a perfect game imo. As it stands, it is still a must-play and top tier game. I just hope I get to one day watch the sun set while I'm going for those big scores at the beachside skatepark.

I was looking forward to the game and was unfortunately mostly disappointed. 
Unfortunately, the game looks like someone tried to program a skateboard game one afternoon but then didn't feel like finishing it.

The handful of skateparks all look the same without any ambient graphics just like the menu, it's just a completely black room with a few tiles in front of you. 

The worst thing is that you can't see your own body in the game. You only see the empty skateboard underneath you and don't feel connected to the board at all. You float over the board as an invisible ghost. This is the most unloving and unimaginative full-price game for €25 that I have ever seen in the meta store. 
The game has applab early access status at best and is not worth €25.

The controls are good so far, of course you do everything with your hands and there's no other way Only the developer himself understands that you cannot steer with the analog stick but instead have to move the arm in one direction by holding down the trigger button. 

The PC / PSVR2 "full version" with environmental graphics was probably not possible on quest 3, so you just skate in tiny little "halls" without walls and without a roof on a concrete floor with a few ramps and rails :-D 

I don't want to badmouth the game, it's definitely the best skateboard game in VR, but also because of the lack of alternatives. 

I no longer have any hope that the developer will continue to work on the game when you see that there are 2 year old videos on YouTube where the game looks exactly the same as it does today.

edit: after months no Update to make the game better... the mobile Version is so bad and Boring but the developer dont care.

I was so excited to play this on Quest after buying it day one on PC. I loaded into free skate practice and I couldn't move no matter what I tried. Both hands gripped the board and if I teleported anywhere it would send me back to the start and all I could do was ollie . This game sucks.

developer_response.body: Hi! We're sorry to hear you're having issues with VR Skater: SL Are you still experiencing them now?

reviews.review_description: I hope they add outdoor parks or more levels at some point. The controls take some getting used to but it's actually A LOT  OF FUN!! I just wish the environments weren't so barebones looking. Also I get booted out of the game during launch 30% of the time (I think standing completely still helps?) But in terms of environments please bring some life to this game! I've seen videos of the pc version I don't understand why those levels couldn't be brought over with lower resolution. Make it a quest 3 exclusive if you have to!! 😇
Very fun but the maps are very limited but everything else is really good
I want a refund

developer_response.body: Hi! Could you let us know your reasons why? Your feedback is valuable 🛹

the smooth controls and learning curve feels so good to play with
